text: O you wrapped ňĻin your clothesňļ!
Stand all night ňĻin prayerňļ except a little‚ÄĒ
ňĻprayňļ half the night, or a little less,
or a little more‚ÄĒand recite the Quran ňĻproperlyňļ in a measured way.
ňĻForňļ We will soon send upon you a weighty revelation.
Indeed, worship in the night is more impactful and suitable for recitation.
For during the day you are over-occupied ňĻwith worldly dutiesňļ.
ňĻAlwaysňļ remember the Name of your Lord, and devote yourself to Him wholeheartedly.
ňĻHe is theňļ Lord of the east and the west. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him, so take Him ňĻaloneňļ as a Trustee of Affairs.
Be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ with what they say, and depart from them courteously.
And leave to Me the deniers‚ÄĒthe people of luxury‚ÄĒand bear with them for a little while.
ňĻForňļ We certainly have shackles, a ňĻragingňļ Fire,
choking food, and a painful punishment ňĻin store for themňļ
on the Day the earth and mountains will shake ňĻviolentlyňļ, and mountains will be ňĻreduced toňļ dunes of shifting sand.
Indeed, We have sent to you a messenger as a witness over you, just as We sent a messenger to Pharaoh.
But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a stern grip.
If you ňĻpagansňļ persist in disbelief, then how will you guard yourselves against ňĻthe horrors ofňļ a Day which will turn children‚Äôs hair grey?
It will ňĻevenňļ cause the sky to split apart. His promise ňĻof judgmentňļ must be fulfilled.
Surely this is a reminder. So let whoever wills take the ňĻRightňļ Way to their Lord.

Surely your Lord knows that you ňĻO Prophetňļ stand ňĻin prayerňļ for nearly two-thirds of the night, or ňĻsometimesňļ half of it, or a third, as do some of those with you. Allah ňĻaloneňļ keeps a ňĻpreciseňļ measure of the day and night. He knows that you ňĻbelieversňļ are unable to endure this, and has turned to you in mercy. <sup foot_note=1248>1248</sup> So recite ňĻin prayerňļ whatever you can from the Quran. He knows that some of you will be sick, some will be travelling throughout the land seeking Allah‚Äôs bounty, and some fighting in the cause of Allah. So recite whatever you can from it. And ňĻcontinue toňļ perform ňĻregularňļ prayers, pay alms-tax, and lend to Allah a good loan. <sup foot_note=1249>1249</sup> Whatever good you send forth for yourselves, you will find it with Allah far better and more rewarding. <sup foot_note=1250>1250</sup> And seek Allah‚Äôs forgiveness. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

foot_notes.1248: This passage lightens the ruling mentioned in 73:2-4. So from now on, the believers do not have to stick to any of the portions specified at the beginning of this s√Ľrah . Rather, they can pray whatever they can at night.
foot_notes.1249: i.e., giving for charity and good causes.
foot_notes.1250: The reward for your charity will be far better than what you left behind or what you have given in charity because it will be rewarded ten-fold, or even 700-fold, and Allah gives more to whoever He wills. See 2:261.
